# Release Notes Skill

An AI-driven skill that produces a structured Vela release notes draft by
fetching commits across the five core `go-vela` repos, filtering and
categorizing them by conventional commit type, and writing an AI-authored
Highlights section.

This replaces the manual workflow previously handled by
[`releases/scripts/relgen.sh`](/releases/scripts/relgen.sh).

## Prerequisites

- The `gh` CLI installed and authenticated (`gh auth status`)
- VS Code with GitHub Copilot (agent mode enabled)

## Usage

Invoke the skill from VS Code Copilot chat in agent mode:

```
/release-notes v0.28
```

```
/release-notes v0.28 --unreleased
```

### Modes

| Mode                            | When to use                                       | Commit range per repo                  |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| **Tagged** (default)            | After `x.y.0` tags have been cut across all repos | `v(y-1).z ... v(y).0`                  |
| **Unreleased** (`--unreleased`) | During development, before tags are cut           | `<latest non-prerelease tag> ... HEAD` |

In unreleased mode, each repo is treated independently — they may be on
different release series.

### Output

The skill writes a completed draft to `releases/<target-version>.md`, following
the structure in [`template.md`](./template.md).

For **unreleased mode**, the document includes a draft callout banner at the
top and should not be committed until the release is finalized.

## Patch Releases

Patch release sections (e.g. `v0.28.1`, `v0.28.2`) are **not** generated by
this skill. Add them manually above the `v0.28.0` section in the output file,
following the pattern used in [releases/v0.27.md](/releases/v0.27.md).

## Validation

To validate skill output against a known release, run:

```
/release-notes v0.27
```

Then diff the output against [`releases/v0.27.md`](/releases/v0.27.md).
